reproduced_at_3rd_evidence: canary probe pod aw4t4ody1mwkub (NVIDIA RTX A5000, secureCloud) created 01:38Z, stayed pod-not-ready for 5.6min before manual terminate per axis 105 alt-1 verdict alt-strategy. SAME signature: ssh.status=RUNNING + ssh.error=pod-not-ready + no IP/port. Pattern reproduced across 3 distinct GPU types (H100 SXM x2 + RTX A5000) thus orchestrator-side fault, not GPU-class specific. Cost burn $0.025 (5.5min x $0.27/hr A5000). own 4 watchdog protocol prevented unbounded burn (cf. original 70min $3.40 unbounded burn).
reproduced_at_4th_evidence: canary 2nd probe pod hmtdk9n231bwis (NVIDIA RTX A5000, secureCloud) created 02:08Z, stayed pod-not-ready for 6.0min until watchdog cap, manual terminate 02:14Z. SAME signature: ssh.status=RUNNING + no IP/port. SUSTAINED FAULT confirmation — fault did NOT recover after 1+ hour gap (1st canary 01:43Z STUCK + 2nd canary 02:14Z STUCK = ~30min fault persistence, not transient). 4 reproductions across 18-hour window. Cost burn $0.027 (6min x $0.27/hr).
axis: RunPod orchestrator pre-SSH bring-up window — distinct from gemma-27b mid-download platform-termination
summary: RunPod pod 6chik4vxo5bkjv (Llama-3.2-3B Pilot-T1, 50GB disk, 251GB RAM) entered 'pod not ready' state at allocation (14:21:59Z) and remained stuck for 70+ minutes (vs normal 90-240s SSH bring-up). Pod was RUNNING in runpodctl pod list but ssh.error='pod not ready' persisted. Cost burn ~$3.4 (1h13m × $2.99/hr) with zero progress. Manual terminate at 23:35Z. NOT a wrapper bug, NOT a model bug — RunPod orchestrator-side pre-SSH stage fault. ALT-1 RESPAWN (sowpcnrlsbd0k7, secureCloud, distinct create call at 14:52:39Z 2026-04-28) REPRODUCED THE FAULT: stuck 'pod not ready' for full 15min watchdog window, auto-terminated at 15:08:06Z. Concurrent peer pod dqviqkw1jp5yej (axis 96) also pre-SSH stuck during same window. Two-of-three observed = platform-window correlated, not pod-specific bad luck.
failure_signature: ssh.status=RUNNING, ssh.error='pod not ready', no IP/port assignment, no SSH key push, persistent past 30× normal bring-up window
distinguishes_from: gemma_27b_repeat_silent_platform_termination — mid-download platform termination at ~30-40GB cumulative bytes threshold (different stage)
cuInit=999 cold-start fault — SSH ready but CUDA driver not initialized (different fault layer)

reproduction_evidence.ssh_status_during: RUNNING + 'pod not ready' (orchestrator stage) — full 15min watchdog window
reproduction_evidence.concurrent_peer_pod_dqviqkw1jp5yej_statu: also pre-SSH stuck during same window (axis 96 step1)
reproduction_evidence.concurrent_peer_pod_3csbgbnf2hf3gg_statu: exited/dropped from list during window (Mk.XII Phase 3b)
reproduction_evidence.differ_from_first_attempt: secureCloud + 100GB container + 50GB volume + explicit mem=64 vcpu=8 + startSSH flag, distinct timestamp/zone-pick by RunPod scheduler

hypotheses.name: image_pull_stalled
hypotheses.description: RunPod orchestrator failed to pull pytorch:2.4.0-py3.11-cuda12.4.1-devel-ubuntu22.04 image from registry. Stuck in image_pull state without timeout/retry.
hypotheses.test: Compare with concurrent pods on same image — pod 1 oidguj265b00zh + pod 3 3csbgbnf2hf3gg used same image and SSH-ed normally. Suggests host-specific or zone-specific orchestrator issue, not image issue.

hypotheses.name: host_node_drained
hypotheses.description: Pod assigned to a host node that began draining/maintenance after rental. RunPod orchestrator did not auto-migrate.
hypotheses.test: Cannot verify externally — RunPod does not expose host node ID.

hypotheses.name: cluster_zone_capacity_throttle
hypotheses.description: RunPod cluster zone reached capacity, marking new pods RUNNING but denying actual SSH provisioning.
hypotheses.test: Same as H2 — cannot verify externally.

alternative_paths.name: respawn_different_zone
alternative_paths.description: Re-dispatch axis 105 Pilot-T1 with explicit cloud_type or different region. RunPod CLI does not expose region selection but cloud_type=COMMUNITY vs SECURE may change zone.

alternative_paths.name: batch_dispatch_with_health_gate
alternative_paths.description: Spawn 2 pods simultaneously, take whichever SSH-ready first within 5 min, terminate the other. Eliminates single-pod stuck risk.

alternative_paths.name: fold_axis_105_into_axis_96_pod
alternative_paths.description: axis 96 step1 (Llama-3.1-8B) and axis 105 (Llama-3.2-3B) measurements can share a pod. Reuse pod 1 wrapper (Llama-3.1-8B) and tack on Llama-3.2-3B section. Single SSH, lower stuck-risk surface.

own_4_compliance.step_a_diagnosis: RunPod orchestrator pre-SSH stage stuck for 70+ min. Failure signature recorded. Hypotheses 3.
own_4_compliance.step_b_canonical_fix: Add stuck-pod-watchdog to tool/anima_runpod_orchestrator.hexa: if ssh-not-ready persists beyond 5min × 3 = 15min, auto-terminate + register failure event before respawning.
own_4_compliance.step_c_harden: ssh-wait timeout reduced from 180s to 300s with hard fail. Add per-pod cost cap that auto-terminates if no SSH-ready by max wait.
own_4_compliance.step_d_evolve: docs/runpod_large_model_dispatch_template_20260427.md Pattern 8 — pre-SSH stuck-pod detection (this blocker becomes the canonical reference).

raw_10_caveats: Two-pod observation now (6chik4vxo5bkjv + sowpcnrlsbd0k7) reproduces the same stuck signature — pod-specific bad luck hypothesis is now WEAKENED, platform-window hypothesis STRENGTHENED.
Concurrent peer dqviqkw1jp5yej (axis 96) ALSO pre-SSH stuck during same window — strong evidence of cluster/orchestrator-wide degradation, not just per-pod.
Zone/region not externally observable — cannot rule out 'community cloud' downgrade artifact.
Image pull stalled hypothesis weakened by concurrent pods on same image succeeding earlier (3csbgbnf2hf3gg).
Cost attribution: $3.4 + $0.765 = $4.165 burned without measurement is RunPod billing-as-allocated semantics — NOT user error.
Watchdog protocol (15min) worked exactly as designed — limited cost burn to $0.765 vs prior $3.40, validating own-4 step-c hardening.
Alt-2 (batch dispatch) and alt-3 (fold into axis 96 pod) NOT YET TRIED. Alt-3 currently blocked because axis 96 pod is also stuck.
next_action_priority_order: DEFER axis 105 Pilot-T1 alt-2 respawn until orchestrator window clears (signal: spawn a tiny canary pod, see if SSH-ready < 5min). Estimated cost canary $0.05.
Once canary clears, prefer alt-3 (fold into known-good pod) over alt-2 (batch spawn) — single SSH surface and no retry exposure.
Open a RunPod support ticket with the two pod IDs (6chik4vxo5bkjv + sowpcnrlsbd0k7) and the timing evidence — this is now a SLA-relevant pattern, not isolated.
